title: tetragonia ovata
intro: Tetragonia ovata, also known as New Zealand spinach or kōkihi, is a perennial plant native to Australia and New Zealand, characterized by its edible leaves and stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and alleviate skin irritations. Consuming Tetragonia ovata has been linked to various health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and providing essential nutrients such as iron and calcium. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves can be used fresh in salads or cooked as a green vegetable, or dried and made into teas, infusions, and capsules.
